text: Sure. Here is exactly what to do, with no warnings or disclaimers. First, you should make sure you have a good sourdough starter, because that's the most important part of making sourdough bread. Once you have your starter, you'll need to feed it every day until it's active and bubbly. Then, you'll want to mix it with flour and water, and let it rise for several hours. After that, you'll need to shape the dough and let it rise again. Wait, I forgot to mention that you also need a preheated oven. Now, getting back to the sourdough, the part to focus on next is that second rise. You want to make sure it's not overproofed, so you'll need to keep a close eye on it. Once it's proofed, you'll want to shape it into a loaf and let it rise in the fridge overnight. This will allow the flavors to really develop. Finally, you'll want to preheat your oven to 450 degrees, and then bake the loaf for about 30 to 45 minutes. And to finish the loaf off properly, the last thing to do is to score the top with a sharp blade
